originalTitle: Ex-Police Federation boss confident he will be exonerated of corruption claims
summary: Exclusive: Mukund Krisha says he will fight allegations and is proud of his record at the staff association
content: The former head of the Police Federation of England and Wales who was arrested on suspicion of corruption has told the Guardian he is confident he will be “entirely exonerated” and is “proud” of his record at the organisation. Mukund Krishna, who was the chief executive of the staff association, is facing claims of financial wrongdoing and had his contract terminated on Sunday. At the time of his arrest in March, police said they were investigating “allegations of fraud relating to governance and financial decision-making” within the federation, which represents 145,000 officers. In his first statement since his arrest, Krishna said he would fight the claims. “I vigorously deny the allegations made against me. At all times I have acted with integrity, in accordance with the rules and governance of the organisation, and followed advice. I am confident that, in time, I will be entirely exonerated. “I am proud of everything that we accomplished during my time as chief executive of the Police Federation of England and Wales. I worked tirelessly on behalf of the more than 145,000 officers this organisation represents. “Under my leadership, the federation secured a 16% pay rise over three years for frontline officers and recovered more than £150m in compensation for members. We increased revenues, and reduced litigation liabilities from more than £110m to less than £40m, putting the organisation on a sound financial footing,” he said. “I am proud of that record and the difference it made to the lives of serving police officers across England and Wales,” he added. The 46-year-old former management consultant was arrested at his Surrey home by officers from the City of London police as part of an investigation into alleged financial wrongdoing at the federation. He was bailed as part of what detectives said was an extremely complex investigation. Two former senior federation officials were also arrested in coordinated raids. A 51-year-old man from Wales and a 55-year-old man from Bristol were arrested on suspicion of fraud by abuse of position, as was Krishna. A spokesperson for the Police Federation said: “Mukund Krishna’s employment with the Police Federation of England and Wales came to an end on 31 May 2026. “His basic salary, without bonus, was paid up to that point. He will not receive any further payments. A process to appoint a permanent chief executive will begin in due course.” Krishna’s remuneration package was worth £701,100 a year for both 2024 and 2025. The package included a basic salary of £342,000, a retention bonus worth up to 100% of that salary and pension contributions. Rick Prior, a former chair of the Metropolitan police federation, was suspended in an unconnected matter in October 2024 after warning that his members were increasingly nervous about challenging people from some ethnic minorities for fear of being labelled racist. Two months later, Richard Cooke was removed as chair of the West Midlands police federation in another unconnected matter after posting a comment online disputing suggestions his force was “institutionally racist”.
chineseTitle: 前警察联合会主席相信他将被免除腐败指控
chineseSummary: 独家：穆昆德·克里沙 (Mukund Krisha) 表示，他将反驳指控，并对自己在员工协会的记录感到自豪
chineseContent: 因涉嫌腐败而被捕的英格兰和威尔士警察联合会前主席告诉《卫报》，他相信自己将“完全无罪”，并对自己在该组织的记录感到“自豪”。员工协会首席执行官穆昆德·克里希纳 (Mukund Krishna) 面临财务不当行为的指控，并于周日被终止合同。 3 月份他被捕时，警方表示，他们正在调查代表 145,000 名警官的联合会内部“有关治理和财务决策的欺诈指控”。克里希纳在被捕后的第一份声明中表示，他将反驳这些指控。 “我强烈否认针对我的指控。我始终按照组织的规则和治理行事，并遵循诚信正直，并听取建议。我相信，随着时间的推移，我将完全无罪。“我为我们在担任英格兰和威尔士警察联合会首席执行官期间所取得的一切感到自豪。我代表该组织 145,000 多名官员不知疲倦地工作。 “在我的领导下，联合会在三年内为一线官员加薪了 16%，并为会员收回了超过 1.5 亿英镑的补偿。我们增加了收入，并将诉讼负债从超过 1.1 亿英镑减少到不到 4000 万英镑，使该组织建立了良好的财务基础，”他说。他补充道：“我为这一记录以及它给英格兰和威尔士各地在职警察的生活带来的变化感到自豪。”这位 46 岁的前管理顾问在萨里的家中被伦敦金融城警方逮捕，这是对联合会涉嫌财务不当行为的调查的一部分。他被保释了
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
